programmes.show_description: Grandmother Rose, who's 80, has fluid on her lungs and is having trouble breathing, and 55-year-old trucker Kevin has serious injuries after his lorry rolled down a verge.
Paramedics treat a man with diabetes and a history of heart attacks who's experiencing severe chest pain, and a 102-year-old woman who may have developed DVT.
This episode looks at how life can change in the blink of an eye. A woman fights for her life after a shocking random act of violence. And Tom, who's 12, has been hit by a car.
Grandmother Rose, who's 80, has fluid on her lungs and is having trouble breathing, and 55-year-old trucker Kevin has serious injuries after his lorry rolled down a verge.
Five Brits compete to see who hosts the best holiday. On the third day in Benidorm, Jason makes a splash at a waterpark before taking the group partying at Benidorm Pride. (Ep3/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Quirky Cornish couple Lynn and Ziggy hope to impress with their two-star paradise, Eden Lodge. But their guests find fault from the get-go, from peeling wallpaper to a dirty toilet brush.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In York, Henry Cole struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers, and Simon O'Brien gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat.
Paul and Michelle want to retire to Spain's beautiful Costa Cálida, famous for its year-round warmth and relaxing atmosphere. Jonnie Irwin shows them what their £60,000 budget can buy.
Liverpool. The first host, 41-year-old taxi business owner Rob, plans a Liverpool-themed evening that celebrates his beloved city. (Ep1/5)
Liverpool. Businesswoman Sam hosts with a South African theme, but her food is hit and miss, on a night of poor pronunciation, fashion faux pas and mischief over the main. (Ep2/5)
Liverpool. Gym worker Toria hosts a night filled with cheesy chat-up lines and flaky flapjacks. Could a kissing game put romance on the menu? (Ep3/5)
Liverpool. Undergraduate Inzy is on a mission to prove that students can cook. But will he be distracted by bickering, dizziness and his flirtation with Toria? (Ep4/5)
Liverpool. Property developer Christine hosts with a Spanish holiday theme. But the mood is more feisty than fiesta: will Christine have to say adios to the £1000 prize? (Ep5/5)
The week begins at the Old Rose & Crown in Birmingham, where paintwork problems and missing milk leave hosts Andrew and Jo Hodges with some meaty feedback to chew over.
Jill and Inez host the second visit at Middlewick in Glastonbury, where their guests are confounded by a cottage next to a camping pod, and by the avocado breakfast offering.
Hosts Jamie and Leila teach their guests all about their family heritage at The Griffin Inn in Lincolnshire. But the dirt is dished about the decorating, and damp patches are a problem.
Julie and Alistair host the final visit, at Crossroads House in Carlisle. The roadside location and mismatched décor raise eyebrows, and Julie has a crisis of confidence in the kitchen.
It's feedback time as the B&Bers discuss paying for breakfast and picky comments about paintwork. Harsh truths are aired before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Lexus IS200: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend delve into unknown territory when they transform Charlie's beloved Lexus IS200 into a full-blown competition drift car.
Datsun 240K Skyline: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend restore a Datsun Skyline, a 1970s Japanese classic. With only two others on the road, it's the rarest car they've taken on.
Three-legged Mr Norman has the cuteness, but he needs to tick all of the Peacocks' boxes. And the already-crowded Sorrell house needs to make room for Turbo Tess.
Geoff's selling up in Spain to build a flood-resistant architectural marvel on a flood plain on the Essex coast. Will he sink or swim when Covid hits and Spain's property market collapses?
...Penelope Keith: Brand new series - As summer ends, storms threaten Mapperton's annual plant fair. Chavenage's sauna opens. And the lakes at Whitmore become a tourist hotspot. (Ep10)
Neil is flown in by helicopter after a head-on road collision, and Matias needs help after trapping his hands in a train door and being dragged along the platform.
John, who's 53, has crashed his motorbike but doesn't want to worry his partner, who's on holiday. Ronald, who's 84, has been bitten by his dog. Gary has a bleed on the brain.
...Penelope Keith: As the summer ends, rain and storms threaten Mapperton's annual plant fair. Chavenage's sauna opens. And the lakes at Whitmore become a tourist hotspot. (Ep10)
Neil is flown in by helicopter after a head-on road collision, and Matias needs help after trapping his hands in a train door and being dragged along the platform.
John, who's 53, has crashed his motorbike but doesn't want to worry his partner, who's on holiday. Ronald, who's 84, has been bitten by his dog. Gary has a bleed on the brain.
Five Brits compete to see who hosts the best holiday. On day four in Benidorm, Exaucé thinks he has the perfect formula for fun, with cable water skiing. The rest aren't so sure... (Ep4/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Singer Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, with a day of white-water rafting and magic tricks. But some guests are kept waiting at breakfast.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn unwanted possessions into cash. Simon takes Henry to a stately home in Somerset, where he thinks he's unearthed a bona fide Tudor relic.
Sandra and Dave want a holiday home with a sea view and plenty of outside space for entertaining. Danni Menzies shows them what their £180k can buy near La Duquesa on the Costa del Sol.
Bournemouth. Bar manager and musician Adie plans a night of fun, laughter and music, with trumpets and drums. But his tricky crustaceans prove hard for some of his guests to crack. (Ep1/5)
Bournemouth. Aristocratic vet Sir Henry hopes to charm his way to the prize with fine wine and good conversation, which is probably just as well, as his food is a bit forgettable... (Ep2/5)
Bournemouth. Country tweed-loving Pippa serves up a seafood-inspired menu. Hopefully her 'strawberry fish' dessert tastes better than it sounds. Will her night sink or swim? (Ep3/5)
Bournemouth. Spiritual Simone spices up the competition with a Bajan menu. But there's tension when Pippa probes Simone about the colour scheme in her student digs. (Ep4/5)
Bournemouth. Flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes to steal the show with her Michelin star-style partridge and black pudding Scotch eggs. (Ep5/5)
At The Grange Boutique Hotel in Cumbria, Jackie and Nigel hope to impress with high-end suites. But Simon finds his room stifling and the breakfast eggs aren't all they're cracked up to be.
The second visit is to Ruth and Lucy's Rutland Luxury Lodges in Greetham. Most of their guests find luxury in abundance, but Jackie and Nigel get hot and bothered about the bedding.
The third visit is to The Leicester Arms in Kent, hosted by husbands Laurence and Danny. Some guests get a spotless welcome, but Jackie and Nigel make dirty discoveries.
The final visit is to Oakover Guest House in Weston-super-Mare, hosted by Simon Robinson and Ann Wilson. The B&Bers are delighted by the décor, but Lucy and Ruth get a frosty reception...
It's feedback time as the B&Bers meet to complain about breakfast eggs, dirty rooms and sleepless nights. One owner's strategy comes under scrutiny, before the winner is announced.
Metro 6R4: In County Down, former rally driver David Gillanders joins Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw to rescue the 1987 British national rally-winning MG Metro 6R4.
Jaguar XJ-S V12: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Wolverhampton to restore a 1980 Jaguar XJ-S V12, a great British grand tourer that has fallen into neglect.
Lexi hopes to bond with Sam and Jackson. Bobby and Milly try to mend Johnno's broken heart. And puppy Cookie may be too much for dancers Ash and Mike.
While recovering from leukaemia, Toby planned a radical new home - wrapped around an ancient oak tree - to improve his health and provide for his family. But building it isn't easy.
Can Zahid and Ferzana transform an old coastguard station on an exposed East Yorkshire headland into a bold, three-storey glass box of a family home?
This episode focuses on a young girl who fell dangerously at a diving pool, and the difficult work of the hospital's security team.
